name: Sourcepoint Privacy and Consent Platform API
what_it_does: Privacy and consent management platform with REST API — designed for digital publishers to manage GDPR/CCPA consent, recover ad revenue lost to ad blockers via value exchange (acceptable ads, paywalls), optimize consent rates with A/B testing, and maintain compliance across web and mobile with deep programmatic advertising integration.
use_cases: Managing GDPR and CCPA consent collection for publisher websites via Sourcepoint SDK
Implementing ad blocker detection and recovery workflows via Sourcepoint API
A/B testing consent notice designs to optimize opt-in rates for publisher revenue
Querying consent analytics and opt-in rate data for publisher compliance reporting
Integrating Sourcepoint consent signals with programmatic advertising stack (GAM, DFP)
not_for: Non-publisher businesses (Sourcepoint is optimized for media/content publishers)
Simple cookie consent without ad revenue context (use Usercentrics or Didomi)
Enterprise-wide privacy program management (use OneTrust for full privacy operations)
best_when: A digital publisher needs consent management that specifically optimizes for advertising revenue — A/B testing notice designs for maximum opt-in rates while maintaining GDPR/CCPA compliance and recovering ad blocker revenue.
avoid_when: You're not a digital publisher, don't have programmatic advertising, primarily need enterprise privacy program management, or have minimal web traffic.

auth.notes: API key from Sourcepoint dashboard. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for consent data. JavaScript SDK for web consent collection. Mobile SDK for iOS/Android. TCF2 CMP registration required. Ad blocker detection via Sourcepoint JavaScript tag. Publisher ID required for all SDK integrations. No OAuth2.

pricing.paid_tiers: CPM-based or platform fee; contact sales; typically $20K-200K+/year
pricing.notes: New York company. Founded 2015. Raised $100M+ total. Backed by Vista Equity Partners, Greycroft. Publisher-focused CMP differentiating on revenue recovery vs pure compliance. Ad blocker compensation (value exchange messaging) is unique feature. A/B testing for consent notices. TCF2 certified (IAB Europe). 300+ publisher customers including Hearst, Warner Bros., Condé Nast. Integrated with Google Ad Manager for consent-aware programmatic.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for consent data, notices, and analytics. API key auth. Good documentation for publisher use cases. Ad blocker detection via JavaScript tag. No webhooks — polling required. A/B testing for consent notices configured in Sourcepoint UI. TCF2 vendor list maintained automatically. Google Ad Manager integration requires GAM account configuration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Consent reads fully idempotent. Analytics reads idempotent by period. Notice configuration reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Publisher-optimized — requires programmatic advertising context for full ROI
Ad blocker detection may require DNS-level implementation to avoid detection by ad blockers
TCF2 consent and ad blocker detection are separate flows — configure both for full revenue recovery
Vista Equity acquisition — pricing has increased; validate current pricing before procurement
A/B testing for consent rates is key feature — requires statistical patience for results

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, TCF2, ePrivacy compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. Consent data is legal record — durability and integrity critical. TCF2 signals must be cryptographically verifiable. Ad blocker detection data is behavioral — handle carefully under GDPR. Publisher user data accessed via consent flows must be processed per declared legal basis.
